mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE is below 4.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising residential housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion, which boosts aggregate demand expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05, or periods of rising INDPRO and HOUST consistently produce negative SPX forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, rising inflation expectations erode real yields and compress equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in a single meeting
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise simultaneously, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding corporate profit bases, which mechanically supports higher equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show no positive correlation with subsequent 20-day SPX returns, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for earnings revisions.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 monthly additions, it confirms robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Monthly payroll surprises above 200k consistently fail to generate positive 5d SPX returns, or the signal's hit rate falls below 50% in a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising revolving consumer credit combined with stable or falling interest rates indicates accommodative financial conditions fueling consumption, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and stable FEDFUNDS does not produce positive 60-day SPX returns with statistical significance, or the interaction effect reverses sign during credit crunches.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained environment where industrial production growth outpaces labor force participation, profit margins expand due to pricing power, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth rate > PAYEMS growth rate (quarterly annualized)
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not positive when INDPRO growth exceeds PAYEMS growth, or the margin-expansion channel fails to translate into equity outperformance during stagflationary periods.
